preview_text:
The model favors Ivory Coast at 63%, with Curaçao assigned a 12% win probability. The gap of 51 percentage points reflects a substantial disparity in forecast strength, though the model gives the underdog more than half its own implied win rate relative to the favorite's most likely outcome.

The draw emerges as the second-most probable result at 25%, making this a moderately competitive matchup despite the one-sided win probabilities. Ivory Coast is clear favorite but not overwhelming; Curaçao remains capable of forcing an even result. The 63–25–12 split suggests a match where the favorite's advantage is real but concentrated rather than across all possible paths.

The model and market diverge noticeably. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both price Ivory Coast significantly higher, at 77–78%, while assigning Curaçao and the draw substantially lower odds—around 7–8% and 15%, respectively. The model's 63% win probability for the favorite sits roughly 14–15 percentage points below consensus. The dashboard makes no claim about which assessment is more accurate; the gap warrants attention from those comparing different forecasting approaches.
